matcher.coveragePreview: number system - session 1: introduction to real numbers grade 9 mathematics students classification of real numbers rational vs irrational numbers decimal expansions the number system: real numbers introduction to chapter 1 session overview subject: mathematics - class 9 chapter: number system (session 1) focus: rational & irrational numbers essential question: are all numbers rational? what we will learn today learning objectives success criteria classify n, w, z, q within real numbers distinguish terminating vs recurring decimals identify examples of irrational numbers what do we already know? natural numbers whole numbers integers rational numbers definition natural (n): 1, 2, 3... whole (w): 0, 1, 2... integers (z): ..., -2, -1, 0, 1, ... rational: can be written as p/q is every number rational? irrational numbers introduction the gap in the number line can we find x such that x² = 2? is there a fraction for √2? what happens if decimals never repeat? rational vs. irrational: the definitions definition of rational numbers definition of irrational numbers rational: form p/q (q≠0) irrational: cannot be written as p/q real numbers = rational + irrational the real number line: fi...
